title: Cleistanthus perakensis
taxonomy: Cleistanthus perakensis is classified within the family Phyllanthaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the Phyllanthus family. This family is part of the larger order Malpighiales, which includes a wide variety of plant species. Cleistanthus perakensis is further classified within the genus Cleistanthus, which is a genus of flowering plants native to Southeast Asia. The classification of Cleistanthus perakensis is based on its botanical characteristics, including its leaf and flower morphology. Understanding the classification of a plant species is essential for its identification, conservation, and study in the field of botany.
uses:
Cleistanthus perakensis has been traditionally used in some cultures for its medicinal properties. 
Its roots and stems are said to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help in treating various health issues.
Research on the plant suggests that it may possess antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential remedy for certain infections.
The plant is also believed to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which could help alleviate pain and reduce swelling.
However, more studies are needed to fully understand and confirm the medicinal uses of Cleistanthus perakensis.

intro: Cleistanthus perakensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae, commonly known for its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues.
